name: Forte Biosciences, Inc.
business: Forte Biosciences, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company whose current lead product candidate is FB102. FB102 is a proprietary anti-CD122 monoclonal antibody therapeutic candidate with potentially broad autoimmune and autoimmune-related indications.

exposures.sentence: FB102 is a proprietary anti-CD122 monoclonal antibody therapeutic candidate with potentially broad autoimmune and autoimmune-related indications.

catalysts.sentence: we initiated a Phase 2 celiac study in July 2025 with the topline readout expected in 2026.
Our Phase 1b non-segmental vitiligo trial is expecting topline data in the first half of 2026

catalysts.event: Phase 1b alopecia areata study topline data
catalysts.sentence: we have initiated a Phase 1b alopecia areata study with topline data expected in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The global vitiligo treatment market size was estimated at $1.6-1.8 billion in 2024-2025 and is projected to reach approximately $2.3-2.7 billion by 2032-2034.
tamClaims.sentence: The global vitiligo treatment market size was estimated at $1.6-1.8 billion in 2024-2025 and is projected to reach approximately $2.3-2.7 billion by 2032-2034 (Fortune Business Insights).

tamClaims.claim: The global alopecia treatment market has been valued at around $3-3.5 billion in 2024 with some forecasts pointing to the potential to reach $6 billion in 2032-2034.
tamClaims.sentence: The global alopecia treatment market has been valued at around $3-3.5 billion in 2024 with some forecasts pointing to the potential to reach $6 billion in 2032-2034 (DataM Intelligence).
